title: Perfect Chocolate Chip Cookies
steps: Brown the butter in a saucepan and cool slightly.
Whisk in both sugars until glossy and smooth.
Beat in egg and vanilla until combined.
Fold in flour, salt, and baking soda until just combined.
Stir in chocolate chips.
Scoop dough onto parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake at 350°F for 10-12 minutes until edges are golden.
notes: Chill dough 30 min for thicker cookies
Sprinkle with flaky salt before baking

title: Classic Banana Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F and grease a 9x5 loaf pan.
Mash bananas in a large bowl.
Melt butter and whisk into bananas with eggs and vanilla.
Stir in sugar, then fold in flour, baking soda, and salt.
Pour batter into prepared pan.
Bake 50-60 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool in pan 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack.
notes: Add 0.5 cup walnuts or chocolate chips if desired

title: Sheet Pan Lemon Chicken
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Halve potatoes, toss with 1 tbsp oil, salt, and spread on a sheet pan.
Roast potatoes 15 minutes.
Season chicken with oregano, salt, and minced garlic.
Add chicken to pan and roast 20 minutes.
Toss broccoli with remaining oil and add to pan.
Roast 10 more minutes until chicken reaches 165°F.
Squeeze lemon juice over everything before serving.

title: Weeknight Tofu Stir Fry
steps: Press tofu 15 minutes, then cut into 1-inch cubes.
Toss tofu in cornstarch and pan-sear in sesame oil until crispy on all sides.
Remove tofu and stir-fry vegetables with garlic for 3-4 minutes.
Whisk soy sauce and rice vinegar, pour over vegetables.
Return tofu to pan and toss to coat.
Serve over steamed jasmine rice.

title: Protein Overnight Oats
steps: Combine oats, milk, chia seeds, and protein powder in a mason jar.
Stir well to break up any clumps.
Seal and refrigerate overnight or at least 6 hours.
Top with berries and a drizzle of honey before serving.

title: Crispy Black Bean Tacos
steps: Drain and rinse beans, then mash roughly with a fork.
Season beans with cumin, paprika, and salt.
Heat oil in a skillet and warm tortillas until slightly crispy on each side.
Spread seasoned beans onto tortillas.
Dice avocado and thinly slice red onion.
Top tacos with avocado, onion, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime.

title: Rosemary Focaccia
steps: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water, let stand 5 minutes until foamy.
Mix in flour, 2 tbsp olive oil, and kosher salt until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place in an oiled bowl, cover, and rise 1 hour until doubled.
Oil a 9x13 baking pan with remaining olive oil.
Press dough into pan, dimpling with fingers.
Scatter rosemary over top and sprinkle with flaky salt.
Let rise 30 minutes, then bake at 425°F for 20-25 minutes until golden.

title: Greek Chicken Power Bowl
steps: Season chicken with oregano, salt, pepper, and half the lemon juice.
Grill or pan-sear chicken 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through.
Let chicken rest 5 minutes, then slice.
Dice cucumber and halve tomatoes.
Assemble bowls: quinoa base, sliced chicken, cucumber, tomatoes, olives, and onion.
Crumble feta over top.
Drizzle with olive oil and remaining lemon juice.

title: Vegan Red Lentil Soup
steps: Dice onion and carrots, mince garlic.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Sauté onion and carrots 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, cumin, and turmeric, cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add lentils, broth, and tomatoes. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 25 minutes until lentils are tender.
Blend half the soup for a creamy texture, or leave chunky.
Season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon.

title: Peanut Butter Energy Bites
steps: Combine all ingredients in a large bowl and stir until well mixed.
Refrigerate mixture 15-20 minutes until firm enough to roll.
Roll into 1-inch balls using slightly damp hands.
Place on a parchment-lined tray.
Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before serving.
Store in an airtight container in the fridge up to 1 week.

title: Spicy Garlic Shrimp Pasta
steps: Cook linguine according to package directions, reserve 0.5 cup pasta water.
Pat shrimp dry and season with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear shrimp 2 minutes per side, then remove from pan.
Reduce heat, add garlic and red pepper flakes, cook 30 seconds.
Add halved tomatoes and white wine, simmer 3 minutes.
Toss in pasta and shrimp, adding pasta water to loosen sauce.
Finish with parsley and grated parmesan.

title: Thai Coconut Vegetable Curry
steps: Peel and dice sweet potato into 0.5-inch cubes.
Press and cube tofu, then pan-fry until golden.
In a large pot, bloom curry paste in a splash of coconut milk for 1 minute.
Add remaining coconut milk and broth, bring to a simmer.
Add sweet potato and cook 10 minutes.
Add sliced bell pepper and snap peas, cook 5 more minutes.
Stir in tofu, soy sauce, and brown sugar.
Serve over jasmine rice and top with fresh basil.

title: Sourdough Sandwich Loaf
steps: Mix starter, water, honey, and olive oil in a large bowl.
Add flour and salt, stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead 10 minutes until smooth and slightly tacky.
Bulk ferment in a covered bowl 4-6 hours at room temperature until doubled.
Shape into a loaf and place in a greased 9x5 pan.
Proof 2-3 hours until dough crowns above the pan rim.
Bake at 375°F for 35-40 minutes until golden and hollow-sounding.
Cool completely on a wire rack before slicing.
notes: Starter should be active and bubbly at peak rise

title: Salmon Teriyaki Rice Bowl
steps: Cook sushi rice according to package directions.
Whisk soy sauce, mirin, and brown sugar for the teriyaki glaze.
Pat salmon dry and season with salt.
Sear salmon skin-side down 4 minutes, flip, and cook 3 more minutes.
Brush glaze over salmon during last minute of cooking.
Slice avocado and cucumber.
Assemble bowls with rice, salmon, avocado, and cucumber.
Garnish with sliced green onion and sesame seeds.

title: Chickpea Shakshuka
steps: Dice onion and mince garlic.
Heat olive oil in a deep skillet over medium heat.
Sauté onion 5 minutes, add garlic, cumin, and paprika for 1 minute.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and drained chickpeas, simmer 10 minutes.
Make 4 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook 6-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are runny.
Crumble feta over top and serve with crusty bread.

title: Double Chocolate Brownies
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F and line an 8x8 pan with parchment.
Melt butter and chopped dark chocolate together, stirring until smooth.
Whisk in sugar, then eggs one at a time, then vanilla.
Fold in flour, cocoa powder, and salt until just combined.
Stir in chocolate chips.
Spread batter evenly in prepared pan.
Bake 25-28 minutes until top is set but center is slightly fudgy.
Cool completely before cutting into squares.

title: Mediterranean Quinoa Salad
steps: Cook quinoa in salted water according to package directions, then cool.
Dice cucumber, halve tomatoes, chop olives and bell pepper.
Thinly slice red onion and chop parsley.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, salt, and pepper for dressing.
Toss cooled quinoa with all vegetables.
Drizzle dressing over salad and toss to combine.
Serve immediately or refrigerate — flavors improve overnight.

title: Cast Iron Smash Burgers
steps: Divide beef into 4 equal balls (about 4 oz each).
Heat a cast iron skillet over high heat until smoking.
Place a beef ball on the skillet and smash flat with a sturdy spatula.
Season generously with salt and pepper.
Cook 2-3 minutes until edges are deeply crispy.
Flip, immediately top with cheese, and cook 1 more minute.
Toast buns in butter in a separate pan.
Assemble with pickles and thinly sliced raw onion.

title: Chewy Oatmeal Raisin Cookies
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F and line baking sheets with parchment.
Cream softened butter and brown sugar until fluffy.
Beat in egg and vanilla.
Whisk flour, oats, cinnamon, baking soda, and salt in a separate bowl.
Fold dry ingredients into wet until just combined.
Stir in raisins.
Drop rounded tablespoons onto prepared sheets, spacing 2 inches apart.
Bake 10-12 minutes until edges are golden but centers look soft.

title: Honey Garlic Chicken Thighs
steps: Pat chicken thighs dry and season with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear chicken 5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Reduce heat, add minced garlic, cook 30 seconds.
Stir in honey, soy sauce, and rice vinegar.
Simmer sauce 2-3 minutes until it thickens and coats the chicken.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve with steamed broccoli.

title: Veggie Fried Rice
steps: Use day-old rice for best results — spread on a sheet pan to dry if fresh.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Add frozen peas, carrots, and corn, stir-fry 2 minutes.
Push vegetables aside, add garlic for 30 seconds.
Add rice and press flat, let it crisp 2 minutes before tossing.
Drizzle soy sauce and sesame oil over rice, toss to combine.
Garnish with sliced green onions and serve hot.

title: Bakery-Style Blueberry Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F and line a muffin tin with paper liners.
Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
In a separate bowl, cream butter and sugar until light.
Beat in egg and vanilla, then alternate adding flour mixture and buttermilk.
Toss blueberries with 1 tbsp flour, then gently fold into batter.
Divide batter among muffin cups, filling almost to the top.
Sprinkle tops with a little extra sugar.
Bake 22-25 minutes until tops are golden and spring back when touched.

title: Cauliflower Mac and Cheese
steps: Cook pasta according to package directions, adding cauliflower florets in the last 5 minutes.
Drain and return to pot.
Over low heat, stir in milk, cream cheese, and dijon until cream cheese melts.
Add shredded cheddar in handfuls, stirring until smooth.
Season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Transfer to a baking dish.
Melt butter and toss with panko, sprinkle over top.
Broil 3-4 minutes until breadcrumbs are golden and crispy.

title: Mango Black Bean Bowl
steps: Warm black beans with cumin and a pinch of salt.
Dice mango and avocado.
Shred red cabbage thinly.
Assemble bowls with brown rice as the base.
Top with black beans, mango, avocado, and cabbage.
Squeeze lime over everything and drizzle with hot sauce.
Garnish with fresh cilantro.

title: One-Pot Tuscan White Beans
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and chopped sun-dried tomatoes, cook 1 minute.
Add drained beans, diced tomatoes, broth, and Italian seasoning.
Simmer 10 minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
Stir in spinach and red pepper flakes, cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve in bowls with crusty bread for dipping.
